response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I spot-checked the algorithmic rules in a number of places and every check agreed with the trace.

• Round 0  
  – Temp1 + Temp2 (L0075 + L0078) gives 0x794CACAB, which is exactly the “new a” reported in L0079.  
  – d + Temp1 (L0070 input d, L0075) gives the “new e” of L0080.  
  – The seven registers were shifted exactly as the SHA-256 spec requires (L0081).

• Round 2  
  – “b” after the round (L0109) is the previous round’s “a”; “c” is the previous round’s “b”; “d” is the previous round’s “c”, etc., so the register rotation is correct.  
  – Σ0, maj and their sum Temp2 (L0104–L0107) add to the reported value; Temp1 + Temp2 reproduces “new a” (L0107).

• Round 28  
  – K[28] in the trace (L0460) is the real SHA-256 constant 0xC6E00BF3.  
  – Temp1 + Temp2 (L0467 + L0470) equals the “new a” in L0471.  
  – d + Temp1 reproduces “new e” (L0472).

• Round 55  
  – The Σ1, ch, the three partial sums, Temp1, Σ0, maj and Temp2 (L0840-L0849) all combine arithmetically to give the “new a” and “new e” shown in L0849-L0850, and the register shift on L0851 is again perfect.

Because every independent recomputation (from single-step logical operations up through the 32-bit additions) matched, no single-bit discrepancy ever appeared.  Therefore no tampering is present.
